studies.duration_additional_info: The VR system is worn for approximately 5-10 minutes during the dental sealant application. The study is a single-visit split-mouth crossover design.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: All participants received both Virtual Reality (VR) and Basic Behavior Guidance (BBG) in a split-mouth crossover design. Total N = 20.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Group 1: VR with right side sealants first, then BBG with left side. Group 2: VR with left side sealants first, then BBG with right side. Group 3: BBG with right side sealants first, then VR with left side. Group 4: BBG with left side sealants first, then VR with right side.
studies.collected_data: Heart rate (beats per minute) at baseline and during sealant placement; Frankl behavior score (1-5); Healthcare Provider Questionnaire; demographic factors; hours of screen time per week; parent and patient satisfaction survey post-treatment.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Mean change in heart rate (beats per minute) with VR: Group 1 (VR right first) = -2; Group 2 (VR left first) = -6.25; Group 3 (BBG right first) = -8.8; Group 4 (BBG left first) = -6.25. Mean change in heart rate with BBG: Group 1 = -2; Group 2 = -2.75; Group 3 = -3; Group 4 = -0.5. No statistical analysis was conducted except mean and range.

studies.follow_up_after_primary_end_point: Yes, adverse events were tracked for 4 months for each patient.
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary and secondary outcomes were assessed over a 90-day (approx. 13 weeks) postoperative period.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention (Standard of Care + RelieVRx Group) = 31; Control (Standard of Care Group) = 32
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The Control group received standard of care multi-modality pain management (combination of medications such as local anesthesia, ibuprofen, acetaminophen, gabapentin, ketamine, lidocaine, and opioids). The Intervention group received the same standard of care pain management plus the RelieVRx virtual reality headset.
studies.collected_data: Visual Analog Pain scale (0-10) for acute postoperative pain; Morphine Milligram Equivalents (MME) for opioid consumption; KOOS Jr. Score (0-100) for knee injury and osteoarthritis outcomes; Veterans RAND 12 Health Survey (VR-12) Physical Component Score for quality of life; Numerical Pain Rating Scale (0-10) at baseline; Adverse events.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Acute Postoperative Pain (Visual Analog Pain scale, 0-10): Intervention mean 4.42 (SD 1.17) vs Control mean 5.13 (SD 1.79). Opioid Consumption (median change in total MME from pre-op to post-op): Intervention -34.8 (IQR -37.0 to -23.5) vs Control -28.0 (IQR -35.0 to -23.5). KOOS Jr. Score (median change): Intervention 32.2 (IQR 21.2 to 42.0) vs Control 27.6 (IQR 17.5 to 38.0). VR-12 Physical Component Score (median change): Intervention 9.5 (IQR 4.0 to 12.8) vs Control 7.2 (IQR 2.0 to 11.4). No deaths, serious adverse events, or other adverse events were reported in either group. (Note: No p-values or statistical significance levels were reported).

studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: RelieVRx: 8-week, self-administered in-home, behavioral skills virtual reality program that trains diaphragmatic breathing, biofeedback, cognition and emotion regulation, mindfulness, and pain education skills. Control: strong active Sham.
studies.collected_data: Brief pain inventory, pain intensity, and pain interference.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: RelieVRx was superior to Sham for pain intensity and pain interference reductions from pretreatment to day 56 (difference from Sham, pain intensity: 0.406 [0.170-0.642] and pain interference: 0.523 [0.285-0.760]). Pain intensity and interference reductions for RelieVRx at day 56 were clinically meaningful (pain intensity: 2.0 [out of 10] points [1.73-2.06], pain interference: 2.3 points [1.99-2.33]).

studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention consisted of a 56-session program. Analyses were conducted from pretreatment to 12 months posttreatment.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention: Skills-Based VR (RelieVRx); Control: active Sham
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention arm received a 56-session, self-administered in-home, Skills-Based VR program for chronic low back pain (RelieVRx). Control arm received an active Sham.
studies.collected_data: Average pain intensity and pain interference scores (measured on a 0-10 scale).
studies.key_outcomes_findings: From baseline to 12 months posttreatment, Skills-Based VR reductions for average pain intensity (1.7 ± 2.1) and pain interference (1.9 ± 2.3) were robust and significantly greater than those found for Sham. More than half of Skills-Based VR participants reported at least a 2-point reduction in pain intensity, pain interference, or both at 12 months posttreatment.

audit.study_title: RelieVRx for Total Knee Arthroplasty (TKA) for the Reduction of Acute Postoperative Pain and Opioid Use
In-Home Virtual Reality Program for Chronic Lower Back Pain: A Randomized Sham-Controlled Effectiveness Trial in a Clinically Severe and Diverse Sample.
Twelve-month results for a randomized sham-controlled effectiveness trial of an in-home skills-based virtual reality program for chronic low back pain.
